---
title: "Analyzing US Census Data"
author: "Avinesh Saravana"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
library(tidycensus)
library(sf)
```

```{r}
#| message: false
income_tx <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = "B19013_001",
  state = "TX",
  year = 2020,
  survey = "acs5",
  geometry = TRUE
)
```



```{r}
ggplot(income_tx) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = estimate), color = NA) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(labels = scales::label_dollar()) +
  labs(
    title = "Texas County Median Household Income (2020)",
    fill = "Median income"
  )
```
```{r}
#| message: false
#| cache: true
edu_state <- get_acs(
  geography = "state",
  variables = c("B15003_001", "B15003_022", "B15003_023", "B15003_024", "B15003_025"),
  summary_var = "B15003_001",
  year = 2020,
  survey = "acs5"
)
```

```{r}
edu_state_ba_share <- edu_state |>
  group_by(GEOID, NAME) |>
  summarise(
    total_adults = first(summary_est),
    ba_or_higher = sum(estimate[variable %in% c("B15003_022", "B15003_023", "B15003_024", "B15003_025")], na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    pct_ba_or_higher = 100 * ba_or_higher / total_adults,
    NAME = fct_reorder(NAME, pct_ba_or_higher, .desc = TRUE)
  )

edu_state_ba_share |>
  ggplot(aes(x = NAME, y = pct_ba_or_higher)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2F6C8F") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent(scale = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Share of Adults With a Bachelor's Degree or Higher by State",
    x = "State-level geography",
    y = "Bachelor's-or-higher attainment",
    caption = "Source: U.S. Census Bureau, ACS 2016-2020 5-year estimates (table B15003) via tidycensus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8)
  )
```
